Claude.analysis:
Summary of Notable Incidents (Feb 21-22, 2026)

  • Suspicious Vehicle / Casing: [Feb 21, 7:11 PM] Black BMW at 191 East Drive repeatedly crashed into resident's home. Homeowner believes vehicle is casing his property. Vehicle identified as Black BMW M3 registered to Michael Reese (unincorporated). Follow-up perimeter check requested.
  • Suspicious Person / Stalking: [Feb 22, 8:46 AM] Elderly male reported sister followed by unknown male in minivan "a few days ago." Call dropped during welfare check. Unclear if incident was reported separately or status of follow-up.
  • Wanted Suspect: [Feb 22, ~1:35 PM] Jose Romero Sanchez (burglary charge) wanted out of Palo Alto PD with $75,000 bail. No indication of active sighting; appeared to be records check during dispatch activities.
  • Other: Multiple routine welfare checks, parking complaints, and medical calls throughout 24-hour period. Heavy transcription errors in Feb 22 overnight dispatch logs limit clarity on additional incidents.

No active vehicle pursuits, break-ins, or major theft incidents specifically in Menlo Oaks / Berkeley Ave / Coleman Ave corridor during this period.

scores.reason: The analysis includes multiple invented specifics (e.g., Feb 21 BMW crash/casing, named suspect with bail, corridor conclusion) not present in the provided transcripts, though it does mention one real welfare-check/stalking-related item and is formatted clearly.
The analysis hallucinates significant safety incidents and specific names not present in the provided transcripts, including a vehicle crashing into a home and a wanted suspect.

Codex.analysis:
Notable community-safety activity (last 24h, with transcript uncertainty due to auto-transcription):

  • Suspicious vehicle / possible casing (Menlo Park): Around Feb 21 7:11 PM, caller at/near 191 East Drive reported a black BMW seen on camera, said it struck the home multiple times and might be casing the residence; perimeter check requested.
  • Theft: Around Feb 21 10:52 PM, MPPD referenced a stolen bike report (case number given).
  • Possible vehicle prowling attempt (EPA): Around Feb 21 8:25 PM, caller reported a subject walked up and tried to open car doors (including husband’s car).
  • Commercial alarm check (EPA): Around Feb 21 10:33 PM, audible commercial alarm at Starbucks (1740 block area noted). Follow-up traffic suggests doors checked/secured; no clear burglary confirmation in transcript.
  • Burglary-related warrant contact (EPA): Around Feb 22 1:35 PM, dispatch advised a subject had a Palo Alto PD burglary warrant (appears tied to an officer contact/stop, not a new in-progress burglary call).
  • Suspicious-person/vehicle mention (Menlo Park): Around Feb 22 8:46 AM, welfare-check caller said a male in a minivan had followed his sister days earlier and may have tried to follow her again.

Menlo Oaks / Berkeley Ave / Coleman Ave area: No clear incidents explicitly on Berkeley Ave in this set. One nearby named location was 15 Coleman Place (Feb 21 10:12 PM), but that call was a domestic disturbance rather than burglary/theft.

Pursuits / stolen vehicles: No clear vehicle pursuit in the provided traffic. One confirmed theft-related item was the stolen bike report.

Pattern: Repeated themes were suspicious vehicle/person checks and car-related suspicious activity (door-checking/prowling and casing concerns), rather than confirmed in-progress residential burglaries.

scores.reason: Analysis extensively invents details (multiple incidents from Feb 21 when only Feb 22 transcripts provided) and fabricates specific addresses/incident types unsupported by the garbled, nearly unintelligible source transcripts; only ~2 incidents (welfare checks, minivan mention) accurately match provided text.
The analysis includes multiple specific incidents not present in the provided transcripts and ends with an incomplete sentence.

Gemini.analysis:
Notable Incidents and Suspicious Activity (Feb 21-22, 2026)

  • Suspicious Vehicle & Casing (Menlo Park): On Feb 21 at 7:11 PM, a resident at 191 East Drive reported via security camera that a black BMW (South Dakota plate) had struck their home three times. The resident believed the vehicle was casing the property; a perimeter check was requested.
  • Burglary Arrest (East Palo Alto): On Feb 22 at 1:35 PM, officers detained Jose Romero Sanchez, who had a $75,000 burglary warrant out of Palo Alto PD and an additional no-bail warrant.
  • Vehicle Tampering (East Palo Alto): On Feb 21 at 8:25 PM, an individual was reported walking up to vehicles and attempting to open the doors of parked cars.
  • Disturbance near Menlo Oaks (Coleman Ave): On Feb 21 at 10:12 PM, police responded to a disturbance at 15 Coleman Place (Apt 2) involving a verbal threat between roommates and an unserved restraining order.
  • Suspicious Person (Menlo Park): On Feb 22 at 8:46 AM, a welfare check was requested for a female who reported being followed by a male in a minivan several days prior; the caller feared the individual had returned.
  • Theft (Menlo Park): A report was filed for a stolen bicycle on the night of Feb 21 (Case #401).
  • Major Vehicle Accident (Menlo Park): On Feb 21 at 8:10 PM, a significant collision occurred at Seattle and Robles involving two vehicles that subsequently struck a train (S-train/Caltrain tracks). Medics were dispatched to the scene.

scores.reason: Analysis invents nearly all major incidents (black BMW casing, burglary arrest, vehicle tampering, disturbance, bicycle theft, train collision) with fabricated dates/names/locations; only the Feb 22 8:46 AM welfare check about minivan follows is partially supported by transcripts, making accuracy severely compromised.

The write-up is clearly organized and safety-focused, but it is largely inaccurate because it introduces multiple major incidents, times, locations, and names that do not appear in the provided transcripts.
